meta_title: Impact Windows in Emerald Hills, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Emerald Hills, FL. Protect your home, save on insurance & financing from $87/mo. Call today!
h1: Impact Windows Built for Emerald Hills Homes — Where Luxury Meets Hurricane Protection
intro:
Emerald Hills is one of Broward County's most established and desirable communities. With home values averaging around $550,000 and a neighborhood character defined by mature landscaping, golf course views along Emerald Hills Country Club, and well-maintained single-family residences, this is a community where homeowners take pride in what they own — and take seriously the responsibility of protecting it. That responsibility becomes especially real every June when hurricane season begins, and South Florida homeowners are reminded that living in paradise comes with a very specific set of risks.

Situated approximately five miles from the coastline and squarely within Broward County's 170 mph wind zone, Emerald Hills isn't directly on the water — but make no mistake, that doesn't mean your home is shielded from the full force of a major hurricane. When a storm like Hurricane Wilma or Hurricane Irma sweeps through the region, wind-driven debris, sustained gusts, and pressure differentials don't discriminate by zip code. Every home in the 33021 area is exposed to the same destructive potential, and the neighborhoods that recover fastest are always the ones that invested in proper protection before the storm arrived.

That's where Premier Impact Windows & Roofing comes in. As 'The Kings of Service,' we've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners upgrade their properties with certified impact-resistant windows that meet and exceed Florida Building Code requirements. For Emerald Hills residents, impact windows aren't just a safety measure — they're a smart financial investment that protects your home's value, reduces energy costs year-round, lowers homeowners insurance premiums, and adds lasting curb appeal to one of South Florida's most respected residential communities. With 40% of homes in this area built before 1994 — many predating modern hurricane standards — the need for an upgrade has never been more urgent or more rewarding.

section_why.h2: Why Emerald Hills Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you're protecting a home worth over half a million dollars, you don't want to leave installation to a crew pieced together from subcontractors who may be here today and gone tomorrow. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, every installation is performed 100% in-house by our own licensed, trained, and insured technicians. That means consistent workmanship, direct accountability, and a team that takes personal pride in every window they set. Our crews bring over 60 years of combined experience to South Florida projects, and Emerald Hills is a community we know well — from the architectural styles common throughout the neighborhood to the permit workflow managed through the City of Hollywood Building Division.

We don't believe in the high-pressure sales tactics that plague so many home improvement companies in Broward County. Our approach is consultative. We assess your home, explain your options honestly, and recommend the right solution based on your window configuration, budget, and timeline — not what earns us the highest margin. That philosophy has earned us an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and the trust of more than 500 families across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties.

For Emerald Hills specifically, our familiarity with the neighborhood's housing stock is a genuine advantage. Many homes here were built between the 1960s and early 1990s, featuring older aluminum frame windows that offer little resistance to modern storm conditions. We've replaced thousands of these windows across Broward County and understand the structural nuances involved in retrofitting impact-rated products into existing openings. Our team handles the full project lifecycle — from the initial measurement and product selection through permit application with the City of Hollywood Building Division, inspection scheduling, and final sign-off — so you never have to chase paperwork or wonder where your project stands.

When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying windows. You're partnering with a locally invested team that genuinely cares about your outcome, your home, and your neighborhood's long-term safety.

section_history.h2: Emerald Hills Has Seen the Storms — Here's What History Teaches Us
section_history.body:
Broward County has been impacted by at least nine named storms since 1990, and Emerald Hills has felt the effects of several of the most significant. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains the defining benchmark for South Florida storm damage — a Category 5 that obliterated entire neighborhoods to the south and forced a complete overhaul of Florida's building codes. While Emerald Hills was spared the direct impact zone of Andrew, the storm served as a wake-up call that fundamentally changed how this region thinks about window and door protection.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit closer to home. Making landfall near Cape Romano and exiting through Palm Beach County, Wilma's rapid transit across South Florida brought sustained winds and widespread damage to Broward County communities including the Hollywood area. Homeowners throughout Emerald Hills experienced broken windows, roof damage, and prolonged power outages. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017 — a storm that stretched nearly the entire width of the state and pushed wind and rain into every corner of Broward County. Irma reinforced a lesson that South Florida homeowners keep relearning: no neighborhood is truly interior enough to avoid hurricane-force conditions when a major storm is in the region.

For Emerald Hills residents whose homes were built before 1994 — which accounts for roughly 40% of the housing stock — the urgency is compounded by the fact that those original windows were installed under building codes that predated modern hurricane standards. What passed inspection in 1985 or even 1991 is not what's required today, and it's certainly not what will protect your family and your investment when the next storm arrives. The pattern of storm history in this area makes one thing abundantly clear: preparation isn't optional, it's essential.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Broward County
section_code.body:
Emerald Hills falls within Broward County's jurisdiction for building standards, and while this area is not designated as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which applies primarily to Miami-Dade and certain coastal corridors — it is still required by the Florida Building Code to use hurricane-rated products for any window replacement project. This is an important distinction that some contractors get wrong: being outside the HVHZ does not mean standard residential windows are acceptable. Broward County's wind design requirements mandate products tested and rated for the local wind speeds, which in the 33021 zip code reach 170 mph.

All impact windows installed in Emerald Hills must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or a Florida Product Approval (FPA) number issued by the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation. These certifications confirm that the product has been tested to withstand the large missile impact, small missile impact, cyclic pressure, and water infiltration standards required by code. The specific design pressure ratings required for your home will vary based on window size, location within the structure, and the building's exposure category — all factors that our team calculates during your pre-installation assessment.

Permit applications for window replacements in Emerald Hills are processed through the City of Hollywood Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, including submission of product approvals, drawings, and required documentation. We coordinate all required inspections and ensure your project closes with a final permit sign-off — protecting you from any future complications related to unpermitted work, which can affect your homeowners insurance coverage and create complications when selling your home. Working with a licensed, insured contractor who understands the Broward County permitting landscape is not just smart — it's the only way to ensure your investment is fully protected.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: The Products We Trust for Emerald Hills Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact windows from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned strong reputations throughout South Florida for their engineering quality, product breadth, and performance under real-world storm conditions. For Emerald Hills homeowners, these brands represent the ideal combination of protection, aesthetics, and energy efficiency — qualities that matter when you're investing in a home worth $550,000 or more.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) offers a comprehensive line of impact-resistant windows featuring laminated glass construction — the same basic principle as automotive safety glass, where a polyvinyl butyral (PVB) interlayer holds the glass together even if the outer pane is breached. ESW products are built with multi-chamber extruded frames that provide exceptional structural rigidity and thermal performance. Their Low-E glass coatings reflect infrared heat while allowing natural light to pass through, which is especially valuable in South Florida where solar heat gain is a year-round energy cost concern. For Emerald Hills homes with older single-pane aluminum windows, the transition to ESW impact windows typically results in a noticeable reduction in interior heat and air conditioning load.

CWI rounds out our product lineup with a strong portfolio of impact windows designed specifically for the demands of Florida's coastal and near-coastal climate. CWI products carry the product approvals required for Broward County installations and are engineered to meet the 170 mph wind design pressures applicable to the 33021 area. Whether your home has double-hung windows, casements, sliders, or a combination, both ESW and CWI offer configurations that fit the architectural profiles common in Emerald Hills. Our team will walk you through the options and help you select the product that best balances performance, visual appeal, and long-term value for your specific home.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Emerald Hills — and How to Make Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
The investment in impact windows varies based on the number of openings, window sizes, frame material, glass package, and the condition of the existing rough openings. For a typical Emerald Hills home in the 33021 area — a three- to four-bedroom residence with 15 to 25 windows — homeowners generally invest somewhere in the range of $12,000 to $30,000 for a full replacement project. Larger homes near the Emerald Hills Country Club corridor with more windows or custom architectural openings may fall at the higher end or beyond. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, transparent quotes with no hidden fees so you know exactly what you're paying for.

For homeowners who want to move forward without depleting savings or disrupting cash flow, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified applicants. Spreading the cost over time while enjoying immediate protection, energy savings, and insurance discounts makes the financial case for impact windows even more compelling. Many of our Emerald Hills clients find that the combination of lower monthly energy bills and reduced insurance premiums offsets a significant portion of their financing payment.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows in this market are among the highest-ROI home improvements available. Real estate professionals throughout Broward County consistently report that impact windows are a selling point that commands premium pricing — particularly in an upper-middle income market like Emerald Hills where buyers expect a move-in-ready home with modern safety features. Homes with impact windows also tend to sell faster and with fewer buyer contingencies related to wind mitigation inspections. When you frame the cost against protection of a $550,000 asset, lower energy costs, insurance savings, and improved resale value, the math strongly favors acting sooner rather than later.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Windows Lower Your Homeowners Insurance in Emerald Hills
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Emerald Hills is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts for homes with qualifying opening protection — and impact windows are among the highest-rated protection features recognized by the Florida Department of Financial Services' wind mitigation credit system. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector verifies that all openings in your home are protected with impact-rated products, that report goes to your insurance carrier and can trigger substantial premium reductions.

The size of the discount depends on your carrier, your current premium, and how many openings are protected. Broward County homeowners who complete full window replacements with certified impact products frequently report annual insurance savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars per year. Common carriers operating in the South Florida market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others — apply these credits at renewal once a valid wind mitigation report is submitted. In some cases, homeowners recoup their investment in insurance savings alone within a few years.

Beyond the wind mitigation credits, impact windows can also reduce your hurricane deductible exposure. Florida homeowners policies typically carry a separate hurricane deductible — often 2% to 5% of the insured home value — that kicks in when a named storm causes damage. For an Emerald Hills home insured at $550,000, that's a potential out-of-pocket exposure of $11,000 to $27,500 in the event of a hurricane. Impact windows dramatically reduce the likelihood of window-related damage and associated interior losses, which are among the most common and costly hurricane claims. The dual benefit of lower premiums and reduced claim exposure makes impact windows one of the smartest financial decisions an Emerald Hills homeowner can make.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Emerald Hills if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Being five miles from the coast doesn't reduce your wind exposure during a major hurricane. Emerald Hills sits in a 170 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code, and storms like Hurricane Wilma and Hurricane Irma produced damaging winds and debris throughout the entire Hollywood and Broward County area. Distance from the water reduces storm surge risk, but not wind or flying debris — the primary threats impact windows are designed to address.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows typically cost for an Emerald Hills home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most Emerald Hills homeowners investing in a full home replacement can expect a project range of approximately $12,000 to $30,000 depending on the number of windows, sizes, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. Our team will provide a detailed quote and walk you through financing terms so you can move forward with confidence and no surprises.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace windows in Emerald Hills, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, a building permit is required for window replacement in Emerald Hills, processed through the City of Hollywood Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission and product approval documentation to scheduling required inspections and obtaining final sign-off. You won't need to navigate city hall or track down paperwork. We handle it all as part of your project.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact windows installed in my Emerald Hills home?
faqs.a: The timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically runs four to eight weeks, depending on product lead times, permit processing speed at the City of Hollywood Building Division, and the size of your project. The physical installation for a standard home is usually completed in one to three days by our in-house crew. We'll give you a realistic schedule upfront and keep you informed throughout the process so there are no unwelcome surprises.

faqs.q: Will impact windows actually lower my homeowners insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits when a licensed inspector certifies that all openings are protected with impact-rated products. Broward County homeowners with fully protected homes frequently see annual savings of several hundred to over a thousand dollars. After installation, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection and submitting the report to your carrier to unlock those credits at your next renewal.

cta_headline: Protect Your Emerald Hills Home Before the Next Storm Season Arrives
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to give your Emerald Hills home the hurricane protection it deserves — with certified products, in-house installation, and financing that fits your budget. Contact us today for your free, no-pressure estimate.
